element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Can gather, build, attack, and be captured by enemy
Require designated mine construction; support specialized units
Famine penalty if depleted; units starve without supplies
Requires officer and drummer; enables tactical defensive bonuses and morale
Reload vulnerability; require distance management; subject to melee counter-attacks
Form similar to infantry; dragoons deal higher melee damage
Distinct tactical roles; mortars AoE kill friendlies; howitzers arc over walls; all require careful positioning

element_introduction_order.element: Naval units (galley, frigate, battleship, xebec, yacht)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Shore bombardment and naval domination; require gold upkeep; crew mutiny if gold depleted
Enable amphibious assault crossing large bodies of water

element_introduction_order.element: Building upgrades and research (barracks, stables, armories, academy, minaret)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Range, accuracy, damage, cost reduction; nation-specific tech trees
Gold shortage triggers unit rebellion; coal/iron shortage disables shooter/cannon fire
Strategic exploitation of defeated enemy assets

typical_relief_triggers: Establishing defensive formations with morale bonus
Capturing enemy units to bolster forces
Research completions unlocking new unit types
typical_tension_triggers: Resource depletion (coal/iron shortage disables weapons fire)
Artillery positioning risk (splash damage to own units)
Loss of elite officers in formations
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Economic pressure cycles drive tension; early game focuses on resource security, mid-game escalates to tactical formation mastery and artillery introduction, late-game emphasizes army composition balance and naval/amphibious coordination. Unlimited unit scaling creates escalating complexity.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundation: resource gathering and peasant workforce management; basic unit production with famine risk as primary threat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Intermediate complexity: formation tactics, ranged unit vulnerabilities, artillery introduction, research tree navigation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Advanced: unlimited unit scaling, naval warfare, amphibious coordination, maintaining multiple resource streams under pressure, unit capture and enemy assimilation
notable_spikes: First ranged unit engagement (reload vulnerability exploitation)
Artillery introduction (friendly fire and positioning complexity)
Naval assault phases (multi-front resource and positioning demands)

notable_level_patterns.description: Mission scenarios systematize resource pressure: early phases restrict advanced resources (gold, iron, coal), mid-phases introduce dual resource bottlenecks (food + artillery materials), late-phases combine famine, mutiny, and armament depletion into simultaneous crises

Tactical advancement locked behind officer/drummer availability and grouping discipline (15, 36, 72, 120, 196 unit increments); creates explicit skill barriers before effective defense/offense available

Mortars, howitzers, and multi-barrelled cannons each excel in narrow tactical scenarios but introduce friendly-fire risk and positioning vulnerability; encourages specialized army composition planning
notable_level_patterns.example_level: War of the Austrian Succession scenarios

notable_level_patterns.description: Large water obstacles introduce ferry mechanics and naval unit requirement, creating late-game boundary that separates land-focused from balanced force composition strategies
